_schema.purpose: Phase 1 spec §7 item 4 / cost Layers 4 and 5. Editorial cost bands for the run-cost and governance-carry layers, keyed to the AI Estate Register's AI-Inventory fields. NOT from TAIL — these come from the Register structure. All ranged, illustrative, user-overridable (D4).
_schema.units: USD per unit per month (unit = seat, FTE-equivalent, or process — defined per archetype), unless flagged qualitative
_schema.register_link: Layer-4 keys off Register fields stage / monitoring / lifecycle ownership (oDesign..oDecom) / humanLoop / channel. Layer-5 keys off the Register's computed exception fields (vendor concentration, contract gaps, exit-tested, NHI kill-path).

layer4_integration_run._doc: Implementation amortised + ongoing run: data plumbing, monitoring, human-in-the-loop labour, model-swap churn. Higher when monitoring is Continuous and when a human is in the loop on every transaction.

light.applies: IDE plugin / embedded SKU, periodic monitoring, no per-transaction human

medium.applies: custom integration, continuous monitoring OR human-in-loop sampling

heavy.applies: agentic + orchestration, continuous monitoring AND human-in-loop on every transaction (e.g. claims)

layer4_integration_run.human_in_loop_note: Where humanLoop=Y on high-volume transactions, the reviewer-labour cost can dwarf inference. Model it explicitly as reviewer-minutes x loaded labour rate, not just a band.
layer4_integration_run.fixed_plus_marginal_note: REFINEMENT (surfaced by the RAG worked example 2026-06-18): for retrieval and agentic archetypes, L4 is mostly a FIXED build/run cost (retrieval pipeline, vector store, monitoring) plus a small per-seat marginal — NOT pure per-seat. Model L4 as fixed + (marginal x seats); per-seat amortisation falls with scale. Pure-per-seat bands above suit only the light/embedded archetypes (code assistant, summarisation).

fixed_run_bands_per_month._doc: Fixed L4 component for retrieval/agentic archetypes (independent of seat count).

one_off_build._doc: One-off build cost band per use case (the capex-style figure a budget paper needs), SEPARATE from the monthly bands above — the monthly Layer-4 bands amortise ongoing run, not the initial build. Added 2026-07-10 for the first-year budget line (CTO review item 2). EDITORIAL: ranged, illustrative, user-overridable (D4); heuristic anchor is roughly 1–8 months of the use case's monthly build-and-run cost at the illustrative volume — lighter for embedded per-seat SKUs (rollout/config only), heavier where a pipeline or orchestration layer must be built.

adoption_ramp_default._doc: Default first-year adoption ramp for the budget line: adoption starts at start_pct in month 1 and climbs linearly to 100% by full_month, then holds. EDITORIAL planning assumption (CTO review item 2: 'simple ramp assumption is enough; label it editable') — the UI exposes both numbers.

layer5_risk_carry._doc: Governance carrying cost from the Register's exception flags. Often qualitative in v1; attach a $ band only where defensible.

high_vendor_concentration.note: Register vendor score HIGH (>=3.5) + critical op → switching/exit-readiness cost.

untested_exit.note: exitTested != Y on a critical-op vendor → unquantified continuity risk.

nhi_no_kill_path.note: non-human identity without tested kill-path → security/ops risk carry.

outside_approved_channel.note: channel Tolerated/Shadow → unmanaged spend + governance remediation.
